sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of okra grown from varieties of Abelmoschus esculentus (L.) Moench (syn. Hibiscus esculentus L.) of the Malvaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Okra for industrial processing is excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of okra grown from varieties of Abelmoschus esculentus (L.) Moench (syn. Hibiscus esculentus L.) of the Malvaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Okra for industrial processing is excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of okra grown from varieties of Abelmoschus esculentus (L.) Moench (syn. Hibiscus esculentus L.) of the Malvaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Okra for industrial processing is excluded.

sections.title: Maturity Requirements
sections.text: The okra must be sufficiently developed, not fibrous.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Minimum Requirements > Maturity Requirements
number=2.1.1
level=3
The okra must be sufficiently developed, not fibrous.

page_spans.text: The okra must be sufficiently developed, not fibrous.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Okra in this class must be of superior quality. They must be firm and must be characteristic of the variety as regards shape, appearance and development. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=2.2.1
level=3

They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Okra in this class must be of superior quality. They must be firm and must be characteristic of the variety as regards shape, appearance and development. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Okra in this class must be of good quality. The following slig ht defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: 
- a slight defect in shape and development; 
- a slight defect in colouring not exceeding 5% of the total surface area; 
- slight skin defects such as scars, blemishes, scratches and bruises not exceeding 2% of the total surface area.

sections.title: Class II
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class II
number=2.2.3
level=3
This class includes okra which does not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes but satisfies the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the okra retains its essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:  
- defects in shape and development;  
- a slight defect in colouring not exceeding 10% of the total surface area;  
- skin defects such as scars, blemishes, scratches  and bruises not exceeding 5% of the total surface area.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Size is determined by the length of the okra (in cm. without peduncle). The following table is a guide and may be used on an optional basis. Size Code Length (cm) 1 2.0 – 4.0 2  > 4.0 – 6.0 3 > 6.0 – 8.0 4 > 8.0 – 10.0 5 > 10

sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number of okra corresponding to the size immediately above or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain okra of the same origin, variety or commercial type, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain okra of the same origin, variety or commercial type, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Okra must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is  allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Okra shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).  1  For the purposes of this Standard, this includes recycled material of food-grade quality.

Okra shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).  1  For the purposes of this Standard, this includes recycled material of food-grade quality.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the okra. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.text: 6.1 Consumer Packages In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
sections.retrieval_text:
MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1
6.1 Consumer Packages In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

page_spans.text: 6.1 Consumer Packages In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

level=1

The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables  (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR OKRA
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR OKRA' e cabeçalho 'CXS 318-2014', nenhuma seção ou texto interno identificável na página para hierarquia.

A página contém duas principais seções: '1. DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' com definição do escopo, e '2. PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' com subseções detalhadas como requisitos mínimos, maturidade e classificação da okra. A seção 2.2 classificação continua para além desta página.

Página mapeada apresenta três seções principais 3, 4 e 5 com seus respectivos níveis até o nível 3. Textos associados estão alinhados com os títulos indicados. A hierarquia está clara com continuação e interrupções indicadas conforme o conteúdo.

A página contém seções principais numeradas 5.2.1, 6, 7 e 8, com subseções indicadas claramente em 6.1, 6.1.1, 6.2 e suas subdivisões. O texto está devidamente associado a cada título conforme hierarquia e ordem. Há continuidade da seção 5.2.1 da página anterior, conforme indicado pelo conteúdo. Os títulos aparecem visualmente na página e a estrutura está coerente para uso em RAG.
